title: Validate Before Commit: reproducibility artifact (code and protocols)
description: Code, protocols and reproducibility guide for the paper 'Candidate Comparability Before Promotion: Conditional Validation in Adaptive Network Intrusion Detection' (framework and gate family: Validate Before Commit). Includes the progressive-drift readaptation experiment runner (drift monitors, the validate-before-commit gate, downstream classifiers), the preregistered symmetric-pipeline dynamic replication (self-contained incumbent/challenger pipelines, frozen vs own transformer policies, seeds 3001-3030, Scenario-A machine-evaluated decision rules), the preregistered size-matched self-contained challenger control (nested 512-in-2000 candidate batches, seeds 4001-4030, ATTENUATION under frozen P/A/E rules), all analysis scripts that regenerate every table and figure, the pre-registered protocols, and a claims audit that verifies every number in the manuscript against the generated artifacts. Version 1.22.1 is an editorial-scope correction of 1.22.0: exact artifact identity in Data Availability, zero-drift scoping of every size-matched claim, conditional (not universal) gating recommendations, nominal sample-size-parity terminology, the preregistered +-0.2/+-0.5/+-1.0 equivalence-margin sensitivities, and a nominal evidence-vs-validation trade-off table derived exclusively from the sealed 1.22.0 outputs; no experiments were run and no scientific result changed. Version 1.22.2 applies five final wording microcorrections (neutral causal phrasing, comparably-constructed-challenger terminology, an explicit justification of the preregistered +-0.5-pp equivalence margin, detector-score scoping, and the 512-per-class scope of the README zero-drift claim) and closes the artifact for journal submission. Version 1.22.3 is an editorial narrative rebuild of the same sealed 1.22.0 science: the manuscript is retitled 'Candidate Comparability Before Promotion: Conditional Validation in Adaptive Network Intrusion Detection' and reorganized around the candidate-governance decomposition (drift trigger, candidate construction, evidence comparability, candidate validation, promotion, future deployment outcome), with a thesis-first abstract, four contributions, a decision-pipeline figure and an evidence-map table; no experiments were run and no scientific result, seed, margin, statistical family or registered outcome (ATTENUATION) changed, and results/raw remains intact. Version 1.22.4 is a final editorial focus cut of the same sealed 1.22.0 science: the main body is compressed (the secondary-evidence section condensed to four paragraphs plus a compact evidence-map table, the eight secondary result tables relocated to the supplement under a new evidence-hierarchy-and-provenance section), the graphical abstract simplified to three steps, and defensive and project-genealogy language removed; the thesis, title, abstract, results, seeds, margins, families, estimands and the registered ATTENUATION outcome are unchanged, and results/raw and the scientific manifest remain byte-identical. Version 1.22.5 is a figure-only correction: the connector arrows in Figure 1 and the graphical abstract were adjusted (wider inter-box gaps, smaller arrowheads) so each connector shows a visible shaft; no text, page count, result, seed, margin, family, estimand or registered outcome (ATTENUATION) changed, and results/raw and the scientific manifest remain byte-identical. Version 1.22.6 is a focal bibliographic patch of the same sealed 1.22.0 science: it adds twelve web-verified references (active comparison/testing, online and temporal limited-label model selection, cost-sensitive and cost-aware retraining, modern when-to-retrain, and the McNemar/Holm/Benjamini-Hochberg/Schuirmann statistical sources), integrates them in ~226 added words across the Introduction, Related Work, Contributions and Limitations, narrows novelty to the combinatorial contribution, and removes the companion Paper 1 reference; no scientific result, seed, margin, family, estimand or the registered ATTENUATION outcome changed, and results/raw and the scientific manifest remain byte-identical. This release's Git history was rewritten to remove AI co-author commit trailers, so its commit identifiers differ from those recorded in prior deposits. Datasets (CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15, ToN-IoT) are public and not redistributed.
